parts.title: The Mystery Begins
parts.text: When students at Riverside Elementary started coughing, Samir noticed the first **symptom** right away — red, watery eyes. Elena, who dreamed of becoming a doctor, tried to **diagnose** the problem by asking everyone what they had eaten. Theo could barely keep his eyes open and complained of **fatigue** that made even walking to lunch feel impossible.

parts.title: The Health Station
parts.text: Elena set up a health station in the library. She told everyone that fruits and vegetables **nourish** the body and help fight sickness. Samir searched for a **remedy** that might help classmates feel better. They learned that the **ailment** spreading through school was a common cold, and it was highly **contagious**, passing easily from one person to the next.

parts.title: The Action Plan
parts.text: Theo worried the cold could turn into a full **epidemic** that would spread across the entire town. Elena explained that good **circulation** of fresh air through classrooms would help reduce germs. She reminded everyone that handwashing was **vital** — absolutely necessary — if they wanted to stay healthy. Some students who had already recovered were now **immune** and could not catch the cold again. Samir made colorful posters about proper **hygiene**, showing the right way to wash hands and cover sneezes.

parts.title: The Recovery
parts.text: Within a week, the school was back to normal. The nurse thanked Samir, Elena, and Theo for helping everyone understand how to take care of themselves. The three friends felt proud knowing that a little knowledge and teamwork had made a big difference for their whole school.

definitions.meaning1: A sign that shows something is wrong with your body, like a fever or a cough. Example: A runny nose is a common symptom of a cold.
definitions.meaning2: A sign that a bigger problem exists. Example: The empty shelves were a symptom of the supply shortage.
definitions.studentChallenge: A sore throat is often the first ___ of the flu.

definitions.meaning1: To figure out what illness or problem someone has. Example: The doctor was able to diagnose the illness after running a few tests.
definitions.studentChallenge: The vet had to ___ the puppy's illness before giving it any medicine.

definitions.meaning1: A feeling of being very tired and having no energy. Example: After running three miles, she was overcome with fatigue.
definitions.studentChallenge: The soccer players felt ___ after practicing for two hours in the hot sun.

definitions.meaning1: To give food or nutrients that help someone grow and stay healthy. Example: Fruits and vegetables nourish your body with important vitamins.
definitions.studentChallenge: We water the garden every day to ___ the growing plants.

definitions.meaning1: Something that helps fix a problem or cure a sickness. Example: Grandma's chicken soup is her favorite remedy for a cold.
definitions.meaning2: To fix or correct something. Example: The teacher tried to remedy the confusion by explaining the directions again.
definitions.studentChallenge: Hot tea with honey is a popular ___ for a sore throat.

definitions.meaning1: A sickness or health problem, usually not very serious. Example: A headache is a common ailment that most people experience.
definitions.studentChallenge: The nurse helped students with every ___, from stomachaches to scraped knees.

definitions.meaning1: Able to spread from one person to another. Example: The flu is very contagious, so you should stay home when you are sick.
definitions.meaning2: Spreading easily to others, even feelings or actions. Example: Her laughter was contagious, and soon everyone in the room was giggling.
definitions.studentChallenge: The doctor said the rash was not ___, so it could not spread to other kids.

definitions.meaning1: A disease that spreads quickly to many people in the same area at the same time. Example: The town faced an epidemic of the flu last winter.
definitions.studentChallenge: The health department worked hard to stop the ___ from spreading further.

definitions.meaning1: The movement of blood through the body, or the movement of air or water. Example: Exercise helps improve blood circulation throughout your body.
definitions.meaning2: The passing of something from person to person. Example: The new book was in wide circulation at the school library.
definitions.studentChallenge: Opening windows helps the ___ of fresh air in a stuffy room.

definitions.meaning1: Extremely important and necessary. Example: Water is vital for all living things to survive.
definitions.meaning2: Full of energy and life. Example: The vital young puppy ran and jumped without ever stopping.
definitions.studentChallenge: Getting enough sleep is ___ for doing well in school.

definitions.meaning1: Protected from a disease because your body can fight it off. Example: After getting the vaccine, she was immune to the illness.
definitions.meaning2: Not affected by something. Example: He seemed immune to the cold weather and never wore a jacket.
definitions.studentChallenge: Once you have had chickenpox, you are usually ___ to it for life.

definitions.meaning1: Practices that keep you clean and healthy, like washing your hands and brushing your teeth. Example: Good hygiene is one of the best ways to prevent getting sick.
definitions.studentChallenge: The dentist reminded us that good dental ___ includes brushing twice a day.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A high fever was the first _______ that told the family she was getting sick.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Long lines at every store were a _______ of the holiday shopping rush.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The doctor ran several tests to _______ the patient's mysterious illness.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The mechanic needed to _______ the problem with the engine before he could fix it.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: After hiking all day long, the campers were overcome with _______ and fell asleep immediately.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The _______ from studying all night made it hard to concentrate during the morning test.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Healthy meals _______ a child's growing body with the nutrients it needs.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Reading good books can _______ the mind just as healthy food feeds the body.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Grandma's special soup was the best _______ whenever anyone in the family felt under the weather.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The principal tried to _______ the lunchroom noise problem by setting up quiet zones.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A stomachache is a common _______ that usually goes away on its own after some rest.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The old cat had a minor _______ that the vet treated with a simple daily medicine.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The nurse said the cold was highly _______ and could spread to the entire class.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Her excitement about the field trip was _______, and soon everyone was buzzing with joy.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The city prepared for the flu _______ by opening extra health clinics around town.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Doctors warned that without vaccines, the disease could become a full _______ across the region.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Exercise keeps your blood _______ strong and your heart healthy.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The library tracked the _______ of its most popular books from reader to reader.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Drinking clean water every day is _______ for staying healthy and strong.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The rescue team carried _______ supplies like food, water, and medicine to the village.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: After recovering from the illness, her body was _______ to catching it a second time.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He seemed completely _______ to the cold weather and never even wore a jacket.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The school nurse taught a lesson on proper _______, including handwashing and brushing teeth.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Good _______ in the kitchen means keeping surfaces clean so germs do not spread.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Dr. Patel noticed that many patients were coming in with the same _(1)_ — a scratchy throat and watery eyes. She worked quickly to _(2)_ what was making so many people sick at once. Most patients also complained of _(3)_, saying they had no energy to do even the simplest tasks.

The doctor told every family to _(4)_ their bodies with healthy fruits, vegetables, and plenty of water. She explained that rest was the best _(5)_ and that the _(6)_ was nothing too serious — just a seasonal cold. However, it was very _(7)_, which meant it could jump from one person to the next in no time at all.

The town mayor worried that the cold could become a full _(8)_ if people were not careful. Dr. Patel recommended opening windows to improve the _(9)_ of fresh air in classrooms and offices. She reminded everyone that handwashing was _(10)_ — absolutely necessary — to stop the germs from spreading. Students who had already been sick were now _(11)_ and helped care for classmates who were still recovering. The school posted bright signs about proper _(12)_, and within two weeks the whole town was healthy again.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
